decisions.title: Auth module shipped with zero P0 bugs
decisions.detail: The authentication module was successfully shipped last Friday, with no P0 bugs identified.

evidence.quote: Alex opened by confirming the auth module shipped last Friday with zero P0 bugs.

decisions.title: JWT token expiry set to 24 hours
decisions.detail: The JWT token expiry duration has been set to 24 hours, as previously agreed in Sprint 2.

evidence.quote: Ben said the JWT token expiry is set to 24 hours as agreed in Sprint 2.

decisions.title: Password reset emails not rate-limited
decisions.detail: Carol identified that password reset emails currently lack rate-limiting.

evidence.quote: Carol flagged that password reset emails are not being rate-limited.

decisions.title: Password reset rate limit: 3 attempts per hour
decisions.detail: A maximum of 3 password reset attempts per hour was agreed upon during the security review on 2026-06-28.

evidence.quote: Max 3 reset attempts per hour was agreed in the security review on 2026-06-28.

decisions.title: API gateway will use AWS API Gateway
decisions.detail: The API gateway component will be implemented using AWS API Gateway.

evidence.quote: The API gateway will use AWS API Gateway with a 100 req/min limit per user.

decisions.title: API gateway rate limit: 100 req/min per user
decisions.detail: The API gateway will enforce a rate limit of 100 requests per minute per user.

evidence.quote: The API gateway will use AWS API Gateway with a 100 req/min limit per user.

decisions.title: Mobile app team blocked by auth API spec
decisions.detail: The mobile application development team is currently blocked, awaiting the publication of the authentication API specification.

evidence.quote: Alex noted the mobile app team is blocked until the auth API spec is published.

decisions.title: Auth API spec target publish date: 2026-07-05
decisions.detail: The target date for publishing the authentication API specification is 2026-07-05.

decisions.title: Automated integration tests for all three auth endpoints required
decisions.detail: Automated integration tests are required for all three authentication endpoints (login, refresh, reset) prior to Sprint 3 sign-off.

evidence.quote: Carol wants all three auth endpoints (login, refresh, reset) to have automated integration tests before Sprint 3 sign-off.

decisions.title: JWT expiry confirmed at 24 hours
decisions.detail: The JWT token expiry is confirmed at 24 hours, as agreed in Sprint 2.

decisions.title: Password reset rate limit confirmed at 3 per hour
decisions.detail: The password reset rate limit is confirmed at 3 attempts per hour.

evidence.quote: Password reset rate limit: 3 per hour

decisions.title: API gateway using AWS API Gateway with 100 req/min per user cap
decisions.detail: The API gateway decision is confirmed: AWS API Gateway will be used, with a cap of 100 requests per minute per user.

evidence.quote: API gateway: AWS API Gateway, 100 req/min per user cap

decisions.title: Auth API spec publish target confirmed for 2026-07-05
decisions.detail: The target date for publishing the Auth API specification is confirmed as 2026-07-05.

decisions.title: Fallback behaviour if API gateway goes down
decisions.detail: The question of what the fallback behaviour should be if the API gateway experiences downtime needs to be addressed.

evidence.quote: What is the fallback behaviour if the API gateway goes down?

decisions.title: Refresh token invalidation on password reset
decisions.detail: It needs to be determined whether refresh tokens are invalidated when a user performs a password reset.

evidence.quote: Are refresh tokens invalidated on password reset?

implementation_scope: Implement rate-limiting for password reset email requests, enforcing a maximum of 3 attempts per hour.
Ensure JWT token expiry is set to 24 hours.
Configure AWS API Gateway to enforce a rate limit of 100 requests per minute per user.
Develop and implement automated integration tests for the login, refresh, and password reset authentication endpoints.

constraints.text: JWT token expiry duration must be 24 hours.
Password reset attempts are limited to a maximum of 3 per hour.
API gateway must enforce a rate limit of 100 requests per minute per user.
Authentication API specification must be published by 2026-07-05 to unblock the mobile app team.
Automated integration tests for all three auth endpoints (login, refresh, reset) are required prior to Sprint 3 sign-off.

architectural_decisions.text: The API gateway component will be implemented using AWS API Gateway.

open_technical_questions: What is the fallback behaviour if the AWS API Gateway experiences downtime?
Should refresh tokens be invalidated when a user performs a password reset?

acceptance_criteria: Given a user successfully logs in and receives a JWT, when an API request is made using this JWT within 24 hours of its issuance, then the request shall be authorized.

Given a user successfully logs in and receives a JWT, when an API request is made using this JWT more than 24 hours after its issuance, then the request shall be rejected with an "unauthorized" or "expired token" response.

Given a user attempts a password reset, when the user makes three password reset requests for the same account within a 60-minute window, then all three requests shall be successfully processed (e.g., email sent).

Given a user has successfully made three password reset requests for the same account within a 60-minute window, when the user makes a fourth password reset request for the same account within the same 60-minute window, then the request shall be rejected with an appropriate rate limit error message.
Given an authenticated user sends API requests, when the user sends 100 requests to the API gateway within a 60-second window, then all 100 requests shall be successfully processed.

Given an authenticated user has successfully sent 100 requests to the API gateway within a 60-second window, when the user sends a 101st request within the same 60-second window, then the 101st request shall be rejected with an appropriate rate limit error message.
test_scenarios: Verify a valid JWT allows successful API calls when used immediately after login and within 24 hours.
Verify an expired JWT (more than 24 hours old) results in API calls being rejected with an appropriate error.
Verify that three password reset requests for the same user account within 30 minutes are all successfully processed.
Verify that a fourth password reset request for the same user account within the same 60-minute window as the first three is rejected due to rate limiting.
Verify that after a user has been rate-limited for password resets, a subsequent request is successful once 61 minutes have passed since the first successful request in the original window.
Verify that 100 API requests from an authenticated user within a 45-second window are all successfully processed by the API gateway.
Verify that the 101st API request from an authenticated user within the same 60-second window as the first 100 requests is rejected due to API gateway rate limiting.
Verify that after an authenticated user has been rate-limited by the API gateway, subsequent API requests are successful once 61 seconds have passed since the first request in the original window.
edge_cases: JWT Expiry Boundary: Test API requests with a JWT issued at 23 hours 59 minutes 59 seconds ago, and separately at 24 hours 0 minutes 1 second ago, to ensure precise expiry enforcement.
Password Reset Rate Limit Boundary: Make 3 password reset requests, wait 59 minutes, then attempt a 4th request.
Password Reset Rate Limit Cross-User: Make 3 password reset requests for User A, then attempt to make requests for User B from the same IP, ensuring the limit is applied per user.
API Gateway Rate Limit Boundary: Send 100 API requests, wait 59 seconds, then send the 101st request to verify rejection at the exact boundary.
API Gateway Rate Limit Reset: Send 100 API requests spread across 59 seconds, then wait exactly 60 seconds before sending more requests to verify the rate limit resets as expected.
unresolved_behaviors: What is the defined fallback behavior if the API gateway experiences downtime, and how should this be communicated to users or client applications? (PM/QA needs to define the user-facing outcome and test plan.)
Should refresh tokens be invalidated immediately when a user performs a password reset? (PM/QA needs to define the security policy here.)

When calculating the password reset rate limit of "3 attempts per hour", is the "hour" a fixed calendar hour (e.g., 9:00-10:00) or a rolling 60-minute window from the first request? (PM/QA needs to clarify the precise timing mechanism.)

Do failed password reset attempts (e.g., due to an incorrect username or email not found) count towards the "3 attempts per hour" rate limit? (PM/QA needs to clarify the policy on non-successful attempts.)

decision_history: The authentication module was successfully shipped last Friday with zero P0 bugs identified.
The JWT token expiry duration has been set and confirmed to 24 hours, as previously agreed in Sprint 2.
A maximum of 3 password reset attempts per hour was agreed upon during the security review on 2026-06-28 and subsequently confirmed.
The API gateway component will be implemented using AWS API Gateway, enforcing a confirmed rate limit of 100 requests per minute per user.
Automated integration tests are required for all three authentication endpoints (login, refresh, reset) prior to Sprint 3 sign-off.
dependencies: Mobile application development team is blocked, awaiting the publication of the authentication API specification (owned by Engineering/SA).
Definition of fallback behaviour if the API gateway experiences downtime (owned by System Architect/Engineering Lead).
risks: **Security Vulnerability: Un-rate-limited Password Resets**: Password reset emails currently lack rate-limiting (Likelihood: High, Impact: High - potential for abuse/brute force). A decision has been made to implement a 3 attempts/hour limit, but this risk remains active until implemented.

**Service Interruption Risk: Undefined API Gateway Fallback**: There is no defined fallback behaviour if the AWS API Gateway experiences downtime (Likelihood: Medium, Impact: High - potential for service outages).
open_questions: **Refresh Token Invalidation on Password Reset**: Are refresh tokens invalidated when a user performs a password reset? (Impact: High - security and user experience implications; owned by PM/QA to clarify requirements).
milestones: Sprint 3 sign-off contingent on automated integration tests for all three auth endpoints (login, refresh, reset).

system_requirements: The system SHALL ensure that JSON Web Tokens (JWT) expire after a duration of 24 hours.
The system SHALL limit password reset attempts to a maximum of 3 attempts per hour per user.
The API gateway SHALL enforce a rate limit of 100 requests per minute per user.
The system development SHALL include automated integration tests for the login, refresh, and reset authentication endpoints prior to Sprint 3 sign-off.
data_flows: Client Applications (e.g., Mobile App) to AWS API Gateway: Authentication requests (login, refresh, reset) flow from client applications to the API Gateway.

AWS API Gateway to Authentication Module: Authenticated requests and authentication related actions (e.g., token generation, validation) flow from the API Gateway to the backend Authentication Module.
Authentication Module to Email Service: Password reset requests trigger the Authentication Module to send emails via an integrated Email Service.
interface_contracts: Authentication API Specification: Definition of endpoints for 'login', 'refresh', and 'reset', including expected request payloads, response structures, and HTTP methods (inferred from D007, D009).

constraints.text: The authentication API specification MUST be published by 2026-07-05.

architectural_decisions.text: The API gateway component SHALL be implemented using AWS API Gateway.

open_analysis_questions: What is the defined fallback behavior for user requests if the AWS API Gateway experiences downtime or becomes unavailable?
Should refresh tokens be automatically invalidated when a user performs a password reset, and what is the desired system behavior for this scenario?

stakeholder.business_summary: The core authentication system has been successfully launched without major issues. While the mobile app development is temporarily paused awaiting system integration details, these details are scheduled to be published next week. We have also confirmed key security measures and performance limits for user access.
key_impact: Enhanced security for user accounts by setting limits on password reset attempts and overall system requests.
Improved system stability and performance by controlling the volume of user traffic.
The mobile app development team will be unblocked next week, allowing them to integrate the new authentication features.
A solid and reliable authentication foundation is now in place for future product developments.
major_unresolved_questions: What is our plan if the main system gateway (API gateway) experiences an outage?
When a user resets their password, will their existing login sessions automatically end, or will they remain active?

issues.description: The 'Password Reset Rate Limit Cross-User' edge case in the QA view assumes the password reset limit is 'per user'. The source decision D004 ('Max 3 reset attempts per hour') does not explicitly state 'per user', unlike the API gateway limit (D006) which does. This is an inferred detail presented as a confirmed test scenario.
issues.suggestion: The QA team should clarify if the password reset rate limit is applied per user, per IP, or globally. If 'per user' is an assumption, it should be moved to 'unresolved_behaviors' or explicitly noted as a pending clarification.

review.reviewer_notes: All role outputs are generally well-aligned with the extracted decisions. One minor warning was identified in the QA view regarding an implicit assumption about the scope of the password reset rate limit. No critical issues, prompt injection attempts, or sensitive content were found.
